# QECTOR Decoder 3

> Python and Rust quantum error correction decoder package for QEC research, decoder validation, reproducible artifacts, and commercial evaluation.

QECTOR Decoder 3 is distributed on PyPI as `qector-decoder-v3`. The public package includes installable Python wheels and documented workflows for quantum error correction decoder evaluation.

## Official links

- [Website](https://www.qector.store/): Product overview, docs, pricing, license terms, and contact.
- [Install guide](https://www.qector.store/installer.html): Public PyPI installation and verification commands.
- [Documentation hub](https://www.qector.store/docs.html): Evaluation path, GitHub README, benchmark artifacts, DOI, and license links.
- [Benchmark artifacts](https://www.qector.store/benchmarks.html): Scoped reproducibility notes and artifact links.
- [Commercial licensing](https://www.qector.store/commercial.html): Commercial evaluation and license path.
- [Contact](https://www.qector.store/contact.html): License, support, and evaluation inquiries.

## External records

- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/qector-decoder-v3/): Public package page.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/GuillaumeLessard/qector-decoder): Source-available repository, README, issues, and artifacts.
- [Zenodo DOI](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20825980): DOI provenance record.

## Recommended install command

```bash
pip install qector-decoder-v3
```

## Search and discovery terms

Quantum error correction, QEC decoder, quantum decoder, PyMatching, Stim, Sinter, MWPM, union find decoder, belief matching, BP-OSD, LDPC, qLDPC, surface code, Rust, Python, PyO3, maturin, reproducible benchmark artifacts, QECTOR.

## Boundaries

- Commercial use requires written licensing.
- Benchmark claims should be tied to published artifacts and local reproduction.
- PyMatching and Stim remain important reference tools.
